title: Wyethia amplexicaulis
taxonomy: Wyethia amplexicaulis is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants in this family, such as its composite flowers and seed heads. Wyethia amplexicaulis is further classified in the tribe Heliantheae, which includes other plants like the marigold and the zinnia. The genus Wyethia is a small group of plants native to the western United States and Mexico. Wyethia amplexicaulis is a specific species within this genus, known for its unique characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Wyethia amplexicaulis, also known as cliff false goldenseal, has been used in traditional medicine by Native Americans for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves are said to contain compounds with possible therapeutic benefits. In some cases, Wyethia amplexicaulis is used to treat conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied. More research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

parts: Wyethia amplexicaulis is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The medicinal parts of Wyethia amplexicaulis include its leaves, stems, and roots, which are typically harvested when the plant is in bloom. preparations: Wyethia amplexicaulis, also known as Wolf's-claw, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. side_effects: Wyethia amplexicaulis, also known as California buttercup, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. intro: Wyethia amplexicaulis, also known as Mountain Madwort, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its clusters of white flowers and lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Wyethia amplexicaulis contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory infections.
